title: EEG and motion capture data set for a full-body/joystick rotation task
description: This dataset comprises synchronized EEG and motion capture recordings from 19 healthy adults performing a spatial orientation task involving full-body heading changes. Participants completed a spot rotation task under two conditions: joystick-controlled visual rotation on a 2D display and physical full-body rotation in a virtual reality environment. The study investigates cortical dynamics and neural mechanisms underlying spatial navigation and heading control in naturalistic movement contexts.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded from 157 active electrodes (129 scalp, 28 neck) at 1000 Hz sampling rate using a BrainAmp Move System with 0.016-500 Hz band-pass filtering. Motion capture was acquired via two systems: PhaseSpace Impulse X2 (31 cameras, 90 Hz, 19 LEDs) and HTC Vive head-mounted display tracking. Participants performed a self-paced spatial orientation task in a virtual environment, alternating between joystick-controlled and physical rotation conditions, each preceded by a 3-minute baseline recording.
